skill.name: tavily-web
skill.description: Web search, content extraction, crawling, and research capabilities using Tavily API
skill.summary: Web search, content extraction, crawling, and research capabilities using Tavily API
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found an installation command, a GitHub URL, and two weak-cryptography alerts. The cryptography alerts are false positives because the referenced lines contain only description text and troubleshooting text, not cryptographic code. The remaining issues are low-risk documentation and network-use indicators for a web research skill.

low_findings.title: Documented Installation Command
low_findings.description: The bash block documents an npx installation command. It is not executed by the skill itself, but users should understand it installs external community code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command appears inside a markdown installation block and there is no evidence that the skill runs it automatically. The residual risk is supply-chain exposure if a user chooses to install it.

low_findings.title: Benign Hardcoded Repository URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to a GitHub repository for examples. No evidence found that this URL receives user data or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is plain documentation text and points to GitHub. There is no code path or instruction that sends data to the URL.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported lines contain description and troubleshooting prose, not cryptographic algorithms or APIs. No evidence found of weak cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text and line 33 is a repository troubleshooting reference. Neither line contains cryptographic code, algorithm names, or secret handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Shown in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: A shell command is present in the installation instructions. This is expected for setup documentation and is not an automatic execution path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is real but limited to documentation. It should be visible to reviewers because it asks users to install a community package.

content.value_statement: Current web research often needs search, extraction, and crawl workflows beyond the model context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Tavily for structured web research.
actual_capabilities: Guides web searches for current information through Tavily.
Supports extracting readable content from specified URLs.
Supports crawling websites when broader source coverage is needed.
Directs users to configure the Tavily API key before use.
Provides natural-language workflow guidance for Claude Code conversations.
limitations: Requires a valid Tavily API key configured outside the skill.
Does not include implementation code in the reviewed file.
Search quality depends on Tavily API availability and coverage.
Users must verify source reliability before using research outputs.

use_cases.title: Research Current Market Signals
use_cases.description: Find recent articles, extract useful details, and summarize patterns across sources.

use_cases.title: Collect Technical References
use_cases.description: Search for current documentation pages and extract relevant sections for implementation planning.

use_cases.title: Monitor Competitor Websites
use_cases.description: Crawl public pages to identify product changes, announcements, and positioning updates.

prompt_templates.title: Search a Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner research on a current subject.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to search the web for recent information about [topic]. Summarize the most relevant findings and include source names.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to extract the main content from [URL]. Return the key points, named entities, and any publication date.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to search for [claim or topic]. Compare at least five credible sources and separate confirmed facts from uncertain claims.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl a Site for Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to crawl [website]. Identify the most relevant pages for [research goal], then summarize recurring themes and gaps.

output_examples.input: Search for recent developments in vector database pricing.
output_examples.output: A concise research brief with recent pricing changes, affected vendors, source names, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Extract the main content from a product announcement URL.
output_examples.output: A structured summary of the announcement, important claims, dates, companies, and cited source context.

output_examples.input: Crawl a competitor documentation site for API changes.
output_examples.output: A page-level review that highlights new endpoints, changed guides, deprecated features, and pages needing manual verification.

best_practices: Configure the Tavily API key through environment variables or approved secret storage.
Ask for source names and dates when researching current events.
Verify important findings against multiple independent sources.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private credentials or internal documents into web search prompts.
Do not treat search results as verified facts without source review.
Do not crawl websites that prohibit automated access or require authorization.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps use Tavily for web search, content extraction, crawling, and research workflows.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documentation says to configure a Tavily API key before use.

faq.question: Can it access current web information?
faq.answer: Yes, when Tavily is configured and available, it can support research on current web content.

faq.question: Does the reviewed file contain executable integration code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains documentation and usage guidance only.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The review found low-risk documentation concerns and no confirmed malicious behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
